sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: CVS Health, UnitedHealth Group stocks tumble on lower Medicare rate proposal
feed.summary: Shares of major health insurers, including CVS Health and UnitedHealth Group, plummeted in after-hours trading following a proposal by the Trump administration for significantly lower-than-expected Medicare Advantage payment rates for 2027. The Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) plans to increase payments by only 0.09% on average, far below analyst projections of 4% to 6% increases. This move, which also seeks to eliminate a "lucrative industry billing practice," is critical for these insurers whose core business segments rely heavily on Medicare Advantage plans.

feed.title: Susquehanna Sees CSX Refocusing on Fundamentals Under New CEO
feed.summary: Susquehanna raised its price target on CSX Corporation to $39 from $38, maintaining a Neutral rating, following the company's fourth-quarter results. Under new CEO Steve Angel, CSX is reportedly focusing on fundamental improvements like lower costs and stronger cash flow. Despite mixed Q4 results with missed revenue and profit expectations due to weaker industrial demand, the company's outlook for 200-300 basis points operating margin expansion in 2026 led to a stock increase in extended trading.

feed.title: RTX Q4 earnings on deck: What to expect
feed.summary: This article provides a brief preview of what to expect from RTX's upcoming Q4 earnings report. Investors will be looking for key financial metrics and any updates on the company's performance. The information will be released upon the announcement of earnings.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (WRB) W. R. Berkley Corporation Reports Q4 Revenue $3.72B
feed.summary: W. R. Berkley Corporation (WRB) has reported its Q4 revenue at $3.72 billion. The company also announced that its Q4 operating income and adjusted EPS rose to $1.13 per share, meeting FactSet estimates.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (ARE) Alexandria Real Estate Equities, Inc. Posts Q4 FFO $2.16 per Share, vs. FactSet Est of $2.14
feed.summary: Alexandria Real Estate Equities, Inc. (ARE) reported its Q4 FFO at $2.16 per share, exceeding FactSet's estimate of $2.14. The company also announced Q4 revenue of $754.4 million, surpassing the FactSet estimate of $739.0 million, and provided a full-year 2026 FFO range between $6.25 and $6.55.

feed.title: Redwood Trust Announces Date of Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Financial Results Webcast and Conference Call
feed.summary: Redwood Trust, Inc. announced that it will release its fourth quarter and full year 2025 financial results on Wednesday, February 11, 2026, after the close of the New York Stock Exchange. The company will also hold a conference call and webcast to discuss these results on the same day at 5:00 p.m. Eastern Time. Details for accessing the webcast and participating in the conference call are provided in the announcement.

feed.title: Ex-Weatherford CEO and Goldman dealmaker join ONEOK board
feed.summary: ONEOK, Inc. (NYSE: OKE) has appointed Mark A. McCollum and Precious Williams Owodunni as independent directors to its board, effective January 23, 2026. McCollum, former CEO of Weatherford International and CFO of Halliburton, brings deep energy-industry and financial expertise. Owodunni, CEO of Mountaintop Consulting and a former Goldman Sachs vice president, contributes experience in strategy, organizational development, and corporate governance.

feed.title: Johnson Controls Appoints Susan Hughes to lead Asia Pacific Region
feed.summary: Johnson Controls has appointed Susan Hughes as vice president and president, Asia Pacific, where she will report to CEO Joakim Weidemanis and join the company's executive committee. Hughes brings decades of operational experience from Emerson in the Asia Pacific region, and she will focus on strengthening execution and accelerating growth opportunities. She succeeds Anu Rathninde, who will depart at the end of February.

feed.title: T. Rowe Price backs Cyclic Materials, Zocks' $45m, and Nvidia's CoreWeave deal
feed.summary: T. Rowe Price led a $75 million Series C funding round for Cyclic Materials, a Canadian advanced recycling startup. Zocks, an AI platform for financial advisors, secured $45 million in Series B funding led by Lightspeed and QED Investors. Nvidia agreed to invest $2 billion into CoreWeave, while Blackstone acquired Arlington Industries and Eaton plans to spin off its mobility group.

feed.title: PPG to supply paint, tech and training to 95+ collision centers
feed.summary: PPG (NYSE: PPG) has been named the sole supplier of automotive refinish coatings for Quality Collision Group's network of over 95 collision repair centers across 13 states. In addition to premium coatings, PPG will provide advanced digital tools, technical expertise, and specialized training to enhance productivity and efficiency. This partnership aims to help Quality Collision Group improve its repair processes and service more vehicles with high quality.

feed.title: W. R. Berkley Corporation Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results
feed.summary: W. R. Berkley Corporation reported strong financial results for the fourth quarter and full year 2025, with a 21.4% return on equity for the quarter and 21.2% for the full year. The company achieved record pre-tax underwriting income, net investment income, and net income, while also returning significant capital to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. Management anticipates continued excellent margins and is confident in exceeding their 15% after-tax return on beginning equity.

feed.title: Pyxis Tankers wields $52M cash, $45M loan for potential fleet growth
feed.summary: Pyxis Tankers has strengthened its financial position through recent loan refinancings, generating $9.9 million in net proceeds and reducing interest rates. The company now boasts $52.0 million in unrestricted cash and a $45.0 million committed loan facility, which it plans to use for fleet expansion. Additionally, Pyxis is actively repurchasing its common shares and has updated Q1 2026 chartering activities for its MR product tankers and dry bulk vessels, reflecting healthy market conditions despite some seasonal softness.

feed.title: Target’s incoming CEO breaks silence on Minneapolis violence near HQ—read his full statement
feed.summary: Incoming Target CEO Michael Fiddelke addressed the recent violence in Minneapolis, including two fatal shootings and controversy surrounding ICE actions, in a message to employees. While acknowledging the pain and impact on the community, he did not specifically name victims, mention ICE, or propose policy changes. Fiddelke emphasized keeping employees and guests safe and stated that he joined other CEOs in signing a letter calling for de-escalation of tensions.

feed.title: USA Track & Field and Hershey Mark 1 Million Youth Participants Through RunJumpThrow Program
feed.summary: USA Track & Field and The Hershey Company celebrated their RunJumpThrow youth initiative reaching one million participants since its 2015 launch. A special event in Indianapolis hosted over 500 students, where fourth-grader Mariah Buchanan was recognized as the millionth participant. The program, which evolved from the Hershey's Track & Field Games, focuses on free, non-competitive track and field fundamentals for children aged 7-12, promoting physical activity and inspiring future athletes.

feed.title: Why Cisco (CSCO) Stock Is Trading Up Today
feed.summary: Cisco (CSCO) stock rose today after Evercore ISI upgraded it to Outperform and increased its price target, citing potential for "high single-digit sales and low-teens EPS growth" and up to $3 billion in AI revenues by fiscal year 2026. The analyst firm also highlighted strong demand in campus markets and an attractive valuation compared to peers. Despite a cooling from the initial jump, the stock's modest volatility suggests the market views this news as meaningful for the company.

feed.title: NTP-STAG Parent Company Initiates ‘Strategic’ Review, Potential Sale
feed.summary: LKQ Corporation, the parent company of NTP-STAG, has announced its board of directors initiated a comprehensive review of strategic alternatives to enhance shareholder value, including a potential sale of the company. The company is also continuing its previously announced process to explore the potential sale of its Specialty segment. There is no set deadline for this review, and no assurance it will result in a transaction.

feed.title: Brown & Brown Mourns Death of Chief Legal Officer Robert Mathis
feed.summary: Brown & Brown announced the passing of its chief legal officer, Robert Mathis. The company and its CEO, Powell Brown, expressed profound sadness and highlighted Mathis's exceptional leadership, legal acumen, and genuine kindness. Eileen Akerson, chief risk, regulatory, and compliance counsel, will serve as interim chief legal officer.

feed.title: Revvity Board Declares Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: Revvity, Inc. (NYSE: RVTY) has announced that its Board of Directors declared a regular quarterly dividend of $0.07 per share of common stock. This dividend is scheduled to be paid on May 8, 2026, to shareholders of record as of April 17, 2026. The company, a health science solutions provider, reported over $2.7 billion in revenue in 2024 and employs approximately 11,000 people globally.

feed.title: AI Bubble Fears Have Sharply Discounted Oracle Stock - Oracle (NYSE:ORCL)
feed.summary: Oracle (NYSE: ORCL) stock has seen a 5% decline since the beginning of the year, attributed to fears of an AI bubble implosion despite Oracle's role as a key AI enabler. This dip presents a potential discounted opportunity for contrarian investors, with current market volatility skew indicating a prioritization of downside protection. An options analysis using Black-Scholes and Markov property suggests a probable price target of $190 for ORCL stock by the Feb. 20 expiration, supporting a bullish outlook on its current weakness.

feed.title: AMD (AMD) Stock Trades Down, Here Is Why
feed.summary: Shares of Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) fell 3.3% after Microsoft unveiled its new Maia 200 AI chip, signaling a move by major tech companies to develop in-house chip designs, which could increase competitive pressure on third-party suppliers like AMD. Despite this drop, AMD's stock has been volatile, and the market's reaction indicates the news is meaningful but not fundamentally altering for the company. The stock is up 12.2% year-to-date and, prior to this news, had received positive analyst commentary and a new board appointment, with strong demand for its server CPUs.

feed.title: Eaton's tax-free spinoff of mobility group unlocks value: BNP Paribas (ETN:NYSE)
feed.summary: BNP Paribas Equity Research senior analyst Andrew Buscaglia believes Eaton's decision to spin off its mobility group will unlock shareholder value by allowing the company to focus on higher-growth businesses. This spinoff is expected to improve Eaton's organic growth and operating margins, as the mobility unit's contribution to revenue and profit had declined and its margins trailed the core businesses. The move will create two distinct companies with clearer strategic priorities: Eaton focusing on electrical, data centers, and aerospace, and the mobility group operating as a standalone firm for vehicle-related markets.

feed.title: UnitedHealth Group’s PR Stunt Disguised as Generosity
feed.summary: The article exposes UnitedHealth Group's CEO Stephen Hemsley's promise to return all Obamacare profits in 2026 as a strategic PR stunt. Author Wendell Potter argues that due to UnitedHealth's vertically integrated structure and the small fraction of its business coming from Obamacare plans, this pledge is largely meaningless to its overall profitability. Instead, it's a "masterful chess move" designed to put pressure on competitors like Centene and Molina, who are more reliant on ACA exchange profitability.

feed.title: Best Waste Management Stocks To Research - January 26th
feed.summary: MarketBeat's stock screener identifies Waste Management (WM), GFL Environmental (GFL), and Custom Truck One Source (CTOS) as top waste management stocks based on recent trading volume. Waste management stocks are characterized by long-term contracts, regulatory factors, and a focus on sustainability, making them defensive, income-generating plays. WM is a large North American environmental services provider with collection, transfer stations, and landfills that produce renewable natural gas, while GFL offers comprehensive non-hazardous waste management and soil remediation, and CTOS provides specialty equipment to support the waste management industry.

feed.title: Telecom Argentina to Fully Redeem US$162.7 Million 8% Senior Notes Due 2026 in February
feed.summary: Telecom Argentina announced it will fully redeem its remaining US$162.747 million of 8.000% Senior Notes due 2026 on February 25, 2026, for approximately US$164.085 million including accrued interest. This redemption will extinguish all obligations, lead to cancellation and delisting of the notes, and simplify the company's debt structure. Telecom Argentina's stock (TEO) currently holds a "Hold" rating from analysts with a $14.00 price target.

feed.title: U Power stock soars after launching tokenized assets on BNB Chain
feed.summary: U Power Limited's stock surged 10.8% after announcing the launch of regulatory-compliant tokenized real-world assets on the BNB Chain, in collaboration with PicWe. This initiative aims to digitize real-world energy assets, starting with battery-swapping stations in Southern Europe, and leveraging U Power's UOTTA™ modular technology. The company plans to expand this tokenization to existing markets in Southeast Asia, Hong Kong SAR, and South America, and believes it will enhance the efficiency and scalability of energy systems.

feed.title: Scotiabank Raises Price Target for Newmont (NEM) to $152 | NEM S
feed.summary: Scotiabank has maintained its 'Sector Outperform' rating for Newmont (NEM) and significantly raised its price target from $114.00 to $152.00, marking a 33.33% increase. This decision reflects analyst Tanya Jakusconek's continued confidence in the stock. The article highlights other recent analyst upgrades for Newmont and provides an overview of the company, which is the world's largest gold miner.

feed.title: OR Royalties (OR): Scotiabank Maintains Rating, Raises Price Target
feed.summary: Scotiabank has maintained its 'Sector Perform' rating for OR Royalties (OR) and increased its price target from $41.00 to $51.00. This adjustment reflects a 24.39% raise, despite aggregated analyst forecasts suggesting a potential downside from the current price. OR Royalties, which acquires and manages precious metal royalties and streams, has seen several analyst rating and price target changes in recent months.

feed.title: Duke Energy restores power to 131,000 following Fern, offers tips as colder weather continues to drive high energy use
feed.summary: Duke Energy has restored power to over 131,000 customers in the Carolinas after Winter Storm Fern, with most remaining outages expected to be resolved by the end of Monday, January 26th. The company is also providing energy efficiency tips to help customers manage higher bills due to continued cold weather. Rick Canavan, Duke Energy storm director, also warned customers about a text message scam related to rolling outages.

feed.title: BlackRock files for new bitcoin ETF with covered-call strategy
feed.summary: BlackRock has filed an S-1 registration statement with the SEC for a new exchange-traded fund (ETF) called the BlackRock Bitcoin and Ethereum Reference Price Covered Call ETF. This actively managed fund aims to provide investors with income and exposure to crypto, similar to its existing covered-call ETFs for other asset classes. The strategy involves writing covered call and cash-secured put options on the underlying crypto assets to generate income.

feed.title: Does Ventas’ EPS Surge And Uptrend Signal A Stronger Long-Term Thesis For VTR?
feed.summary: Ventas (NYSE: VTR) has shown rapid earnings per share expansion, steady revenue growth, and rising free cash flow, consistently beating analyst expectations. This performance, along with a confirmed upward stock trend and tight consolidation, suggests growing investor confidence in the company's long-term prospects. However, the article highlights inherent risks related to valuation, dividend coverage, and sensitivity to future growth disappointments, emphasizing the need for investors to consider various viewpoints.

feed.title: Target drops off Fortune's Most Admired Companies list after 24 years as an All-Star
feed.summary: Target has been removed from Fortune magazine's World's Most Admired Companies All-Star list, ending a nearly 25-year streak. This marks the first time since 2002 that the retail giant has not appeared on the prestigious corporate reputation ranking.

feed.title: Precision Drilling (TSE:PD) Price Target Raised to C$118.00 at TD Securities
feed.summary: TD Securities has increased its price target for Precision Drilling (TSE:PD) from C$80.00 to C$118.00, suggesting a potential upside of 10.89% from its previous closing price. This adjustment is part of a broader trend, with several other analysts also raising their targets, resulting in a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating and an average target price of C$116.86. Shares of Precision Drilling were trading near their 12-month high at C$106.41, reflecting positive market momentum for the North American oil and gas services provider.

feed.title: Guarantor: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC has issued $2,665,000 in Review Notes linked to the performance of the S&P 500 Index, Nasdaq-100 Index, and the State Street SPDR S&P Regional Banking ETF, with JPMorgan Chase & Co. fully and unconditionally guaranteeing the payments. These structured investments are designed for investors seeking an early exit at a premium if specific market conditions are met, but they carry significant risks including potential loss of principal and credit risk associated with the issuer and guarantor. The offering outlines key terms, hypothetical payout scenarios, and various risk considerations, including those related to market volatility and limited liquidity.

feed.title: ON24 integrates digital tools to capture buyer attention
feed.summary: ON24 is focusing on leveraging digital engagement tools to help businesses capture buyer attention and interact with consumers on their own schedule. The company emphasizes the importance of effective engagement at every touchpoint in today's digital landscape. This strategy aligns with their ongoing efforts to address industry needs, including previous initiatives to tackle scalability challenges in life sciences webinars, reinforcing the role of adaptive engagement technologies across various sectors.

feed.title: The Industrial REIT Prologis Is a Data Center Play. Why Some Analysts Don’t See It as a Buy.
feed.summary: Prologis, an industrial REIT, recently reported a solid December quarter. Analysts expressed significant interest in the company's venture into AI data centers during the earnings call. Some analysts, however, do not view it as a buy despite its position in this emerging market.

feed.title: Micron Technology Shares Drop Amid Competitive Pressure
feed.summary: Micron Technology shares dropped 1.99% following reports of Samsung nearing Nvidia certification for its HBM4 AI memory chips, increasing competitive pressure. Despite this, analysts maintain a positive outlook for Micron, rating it a "Strong Buy" due to strong growth, profitability, and momentum, anticipating a "super-upcycle" driven by AI demand and plans for significant CapEx expansion. While there's a projected 1-year price target fall according to Wall Street analysts, their current ratings largely remain positive with several recent upgrades and initiations.

feed.title: UNFI unveils dozens of new private-label items
feed.summary: United Natural Foods Inc. (UNFI) is introducing nearly 50 new private label products this month, targeting snacking, health and wellness, and value categories under brands like Essential Everyday and Woodstock. This move aligns with a broader industry trend where private label sales are growing faster than national brands due to increased consumer and retailer embrace, especially with rising food costs and a focus on wellness. UNFI's private brands program, already valued at over $1 billion, is strategically positioned for further innovation and growth, particularly within the natural products space.

feed.title: It Might Be Time to Stream This Netflix ETF
feed.summary: The article discusses the Direxion Daily NFLX Bull 2X Shares (NFXL) ETF, suggesting it could present an opportunity if Netflix's stock (NFLX) rebounds. This potential rebound hinges on Netflix successfully integrating its acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery assets and demonstrating strong international subscriber growth and better-than-expected cash flow in 2026. The NFXL ETF aims to deliver 200% of Netflix's daily performance.

feed.title: Plug Power Stock Is Sliding Monday: What's Going On?
feed.summary: Plug Power (NASDAQ: PLUG) shares declined on Monday, despite the company's recent announcement of completing 100 MW of PEM GenEco electrolyzer installations at Galp's Sines Refinery in Europe. This project aims to produce 15,000 tons of renewable hydrogen annually, reducing the refinery's emissions. Analysts' ratings for PLUG are mixed, with a "Hold" consensus and an average price target of $2.38.

feed.title: Caterpillar Q4 Preview: Backlog, Margins, And The AI Power Trade (NYSE:CAT)
feed.summary: This article initiates coverage on Caterpillar Inc. (CAT) with a Buy rating ahead of its Q4 2025 earnings report. The author highlights the strong performance of the Energy & Transportation segment, significant backlog growth, and concerns about profitability due to tariffs. The rating is based on a multi-year outlook, acknowledging recent market volatility.

feed.title: Jefferies Financial Group Forecasts Strong Price Appreciation for Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) Stock
feed.summary: Jefferies Financial Group raised its price target for Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) from $42 to $45, maintaining a "hold" rating and implying a 6.9% upside. This follows similar price target increases from other brokerages, though the consensus rating for LUV remains "Hold" with an average price target of $42.37. The stock recently traded at $42.09, with a market cap of $21.77 billion and a P/E ratio of 64.55.

feed.title: Arlington cybersecurity firm Shift5 names L3Harris veteran as interim CEO as founder steps back
feed.summary: Shift5, an Arlington-based cybersecurity firm, has appointed L3Harris veteran Toby Magsig as its interim CEO as founder Josh Lospinoso steps back from the role. The company has brought in Magsig to lead day-to-day operations. This move follows the startup's recent success in securing significant venture capital funding.

feed.title: Black Music Action Coalition, Student Freedom Initiative and Live Nation Urban Launch Paid Live Music Accelerator for HBCU Students at HBCU AWAREFEST
feed.summary: The Black Music Action Coalition (BMAC), Student Freedom Initiative (SFI), and Live Nation Urban have partnered to launch a paid live music accelerator program for Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCU) students at HBCU AWAREFEST. Fifteen students will gain hands-on experience in various aspects of live entertainment, including production, marketing, and event management, while earning income. This initiative aims to create pathways and opportunities for HBCU students in the live music industry, combating student debt and fostering skill development.

feed.title: Deep Dive Into SiTime Stock: Analyst Perspectives (8 Ratings)
feed.summary: Eight analysts have rated SiTime (NASDAQ: SITM), with a majority being bullish or somewhat bullish. The average 12-month price target for SiTime is $369.38, reflecting a 21.77% increase from the previous average. SiTime exhibits strong revenue growth and net margin, but faces challenges with its return on equity and assets.

feed.title: World’s largest steam locomotive to cross U.S. for America’s 250th birthday
feed.summary: Union Pacific's Big Boy No. 4014, the world’s largest operating steam locomotive, will embark on its first-ever coast-to-coast tour to celebrate America’s 250th birthday. The tour, featuring a new commemorative locomotive No. 1776 – America250, will highlight the vital role railroads have played in unifying the nation. The first leg of the tour begins March 29 from Cheyenne, Wyoming, traveling west, with an eastern leg anticipated to start in late spring.

feed.title: Inside New Zealand’s push to keep AI data onshore with HPE and 2degrees
feed.summary: HPE and 2degrees are collaborating to build a private AI platform in New Zealand, aiming to accelerate AI-driven innovation while ensuring data sovereignty. This partnership will keep critical customer and operational data onshore, improving reliability, network performance, and enabling faster development of new services. The initiative addresses complex data regulations and strengthens New Zealand's digital future by embedding AI securely across 2degrees' operations for autonomous network management, predictive maintenance, and capacity planning.

feed.title: F5 Faces Class Action Lawsuit Over Cybersecurity Incident
feed.summary: F5 (FFIV) is facing a class-action lawsuit following a significant cybersecurity incident discovered in August 2025, which led to a substantial drop in stock price and a lowered revenue growth forecast for 2026. Hagens Berman is investigating the company's disclosure timing amidst investor concerns regarding transparency. The lawsuit's lead plaintiff deadline is February 17, 2026, offering legal recourse for investors who purchased F5 securities between October 28, 2024, and October 27, 2025, to seek legal remedies.

feed.title: Takeda Pharmaceutical (NYSE:TAK) Sets New 52-Week High - What's Next?
feed.summary: Takeda Pharmaceutical (NYSE:TAK) recently hit a new 52-week high of $16.58, trading above its 50-day and 200-day moving averages. While Morgan Stanley initiated an "overweight" rating, the consensus among analysts remains a "Hold." The company reported Q3 earnings that met EPS estimates but missed revenue expectations, and it has issued FY2025 EPS guidance of 3.25.

feed.title: Teradyne announces $0.12 quarterly dividend payable in March
feed.summary: Teradyne, Inc. (NASDAQ:TER) has announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share, payable on March 13, 2026, continuing its tradition of consistent dividend distributions. The announcement follows strong third-quarter results driven by AI-related demand, leading to analyst upgrades and plans for a new robotics operations hub in Michigan. The company designs and produces testing solutions for semiconductors and electronics, as well as collaborative and mobile robots.

feed.title: NRG Energy receives final antitrust clearance for LS Power asset acquisition
feed.summary: NRG Energy has received final antitrust clearance from the U.S. Department of Justice for its acquisition of 18 natural gas generation facilities and a virtual power plant platform from LS Power. This clearance, following prior approvals, paves the way for the transaction's imminent closure, adding 13 gigawatts of quick-start natural gas-fired capacity and 6 gigawatts of virtual power plant capability to NRG's portfolio. The acquisition is expected to enhance NRG's scale, resilience, and reliability amidst increasing power demand.
